---
title: "2.20.26Cleaning"
author: "Kaitlin Rommelfanger"
date: "2026-02-20"
output: html_document
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r setup}
library(readr)
library(tidyverse)
nursery <- read_csv("202026CleaningCheck.csv")

nursery$`Monitoring Session`<-as.factor(nursery$`Monitoring Session`)
nursery$Location <- as.factor(nursery$Location)
nursery$Treatment<- as.factor(nursery$Treatment)
nursery$`Dead/Alive/Dying`<-as.factor(nursery$`Dead/Alive/Dying`)
nursery$`Epicotyl length (cm)`<-as.numeric(nursery$`Epicotyl length (cm)`)

unique(nursery$Treatment)
unique(nursery$`Dead/Alive/Dying`)
unique(nursery$`Monitoring Session`)
nursery$`Dead/Alive/Dying`<-as.factor(nursery$`Dead/Alive/Dying`)




#look at number alive over time
survivorship<-nursery%>%
  group_by(Treatment, `Dead/Alive/Dying`,`Monitoring Session`)%>%
  summarise(count=n())%>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = `Monitoring Session`, values_from = count)

#look at number of plants monitored in each montoring session 
moncheck<-nursery%>%
  group_by(`Monitoring Session`,`Propagule ID`)%>%
  summarise(n=n())%>%
  filter(n>1)

#fix problem children that were monitored twice, then were gonna check and make sure that we have the same number or trees in each mon sesh in each treatment


#Treatment, Monitoring Session
treatmentcheck<-nursery%>%
  group_by(Treatment, `Monitoring Session`)%>%
  summarise(n=n())%>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = 'Monitoring Session', values_from = n)


  
#number of mon sesh for each tree
montree<-nursery%>%
  group_by(`Propagule ID`)%>%
  summarise(length(unique(`Monitoring Session`)))


```
